summary_strategy: Position Conner as a security-focused generalist engineer who builds production-grade automation and tooling—emphasizing cross-platform systems thinking, deployable controls, and measurable risk reduction—while acknowledging the absence of Android-specific experience without apology
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line skills format to group core competencies; lead with Security Engineering & Automation, then Cloud/Platform Integrations (highlighting AWS ECS, Docker, CI/CD), and finally Security Data/AI to align with DevSecOps mindset; omit Android-related categories entirely

experience_focus.must_highlight: deployable security controls, cross-platform automation (macOS/Windows/Azure), API-driven remediation
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 7,000+ endpoints, 10,000+ devices, 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: Any mention of mobile development, Android SDK, Java/Kotlin for Android, or unverified scale metrics like '25 percent improvement' unless explicitly sourced in resume text

risk_controls: Do not invent Android experience—omit the requirement entirely rather than imply transferability
Remove all percentage-based outcomes unless directly quoted in original resume bullets (e.g., '25 percent' appears only under Great Wolf—keep only if verifiable)
Replace vague phrases like 'accelerated patch deployment' with concrete actions: 'built API-driven vulnerability response automation for macOS/Windows fleet'
Never assume metrics beyond what's explicitly stated in the baseline resume
